slides.title: Your 3pm coffee is *wrecking your sleep*.
slides.sub: Caffeine has a long tail — half of it can still be in your system at bedtime.

slides.title: Caffeine has a half-life
slides.body: It takes about *5–6 hours* to clear half a dose. A 3pm cup is still working at 9pm.

slides.title: Stop by early afternoon
slides.body: A simple rule: *no caffeine after 2pm*. It protects the deep sleep that actually restores your energy.

slides.title: You won't feel it
slides.body: You can fall asleep fine and still lose deep sleep. The cost shows up as *next-day fatigue*.

slides.title: Trade the late cup
slides.body: Crave the ritual? *Decaf or herbal tea* gives you the warm-mug habit without the 9pm jolt.

slides.title: Taper, don't quit
slides.body: Move your last cup *30 minutes earlier* each week. Painless, and your sleep notices fast.

slides.title: Time caffeine *the smart way*.
slides.body: Log your coffee in Vibe Health and watch how the timing moves your sleep score the next morning.

caption:
Love an afternoon coffee but toss and turn at night? They're connected. ☕

Caffeine's half-life is 5–6 hours, so a 3pm cup is still in you at 9pm. Try a hard 2pm cutoff and protect your deep sleep. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health connects your coffee, sleep and energy into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: When to Stop Drinking Coffee for Better Sleep
